spikeMethod: node spike.mjs (single-shot stdin write, hold for ≤5s waiting for first response line)

wireObservations.jsonRpcVersionField: absent — codex app-server uses JSON-RPC lite without the `jsonrpc` envelope field
wireObservations.idType: echoes whatever client sent — observed both number (case 1) and string (case 2) work; client should default to monotonic number for outgoing requests
wireObservations.errorDataField: absent in 0.125.0 — both unknown-method and invalid-params errors return only {code, message}
wireObservations.errorCodeCollision: Codex 0.125.0 collapses unknown-method and invalid-params into the same -32600 code. Client must parse error.message to distinguish — keyword `unknown variant` for unknown-method vs `missing field`/`invalid type` for invalid-params.
wireObservations.malformedJsonBehavior: no JSON-RPC error response is sent on stdout. Codex writes a tracing log line to stderr with ANSI color codes. Client must treat stderr as plaintext, never JSON.
wireObservations.initializeResponseShape: { userAgent, codexHome, platformFamily, platformOs } — note `platformFamily` and `platformOs` are SPLIT, not a single `platform` field as 05-PROTOCOL.md older drafts may suggest

fixtures.initialize-response.jsonl: Case 1: numeric id initialize round-trip. Single response line.
fixtures.string-id-initialize-response.jsonl: Case 2: string id initialize. Confirms server echoes string IDs verbatim.
fixtures.unknown-method-error.jsonl: Case 3: unknown method `does/not/exist`. Returns -32600 with full method registry enumerated in error.message.
fixtures.invalid-params-error.jsonl: Case 4: initialize with wrong params shape. Returns -32600 + `missing field clientInfo`.
fixtures.malformed-json.stderr.txt: Case 5: non-JSON input. Empty stdout, ANSI-decorated tracing log on stderr.

notes: id type observed: number (case 1) AND string (case 2) both accepted
jsonrpc field observed: absent — wire is JSON-RPC lite
error.data observed: absent on 0.125.0 (both -32600 cases lack data)
malformed JSON: no JSON-RPC error response, stderr only (with ANSI color codes)

approval method names from old 05-PROTOCOL.md are stale; the real names are item/{commandExecution,fileChange,permissions,tool}/{requestApproval,requestUserInput} on the server-initiated side, plus thread/approveGuardianDeniedAction on the client side
codex 0.125.0 client method registry leaked verbatim in case-3 error.message — usable as a runtime sanity probe
InitializeResult shape uses platformFamily + platformOs split, not single platform field
